name: Montánchez from Trujillo – circular by the Castles – the Sierras de Montánchez
name_original: Montánchez desde Trujillo – circular por los Castillos – las Sierras de Montánchez
region_intro: Road cycling around Trujillo, Spain, offers diverse terrain characterized by rolling hills, expansive valleys, and Mediterranean woodlands known as dehesa. The region features a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents, with routes often passing through historical sites and natural parks. This landscape provides varied road cycling experiences, from routes through ancient towns to those bordering wild natural reserves. Best road cycling routes around Trujillo The most popular road cycling route is Montánchez from Trujillo – circular by the Castles – the Sierras de Montánchez, a difficult 89.0 miles (143.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route explores the Sierra de Montánchez, passing by historical castles. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Trujillo and Monfragüe National Park Loop, a difficult 107.4 miles (172.9 km) path. This extensive route offers views of Monfragüe National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve known for its Mediterranean mountain landscape. Local road cyclists also love the The Santa Ana Reservoir and The Castle of Monroy, a 63.2 miles (101.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a reservoir and a historic castle, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Trujillo is defined by rolling hills, historical sites, and the proximity to Monfragüe National Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Trujillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Trujillo?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Trujillo, offering a variety of experiences. These include 1 easy route, 14 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Trujillo?
A: Road cycling around Trujillo features diverse terrain, characterized by rolling hills, expansive valleys, and Mediterranean woodlands known as dehesa. You'll find a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents, with routes often passing through historical sites and natural parks.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes near Trujillo?
A: Yes, Trujillo offers several circular road cycling routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058713447/montanchez-from-trujillo-circular-by-the-castles-the-sierras-de-montanchez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez from Trujillo – circular by the Castles – the Sierras de Montánchez&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 143.3 km trail exploring the Sierra de Montánchez. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350462516/circular-route-of-trujillo-la-aldea-del-obispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of Trujillo, La Aldea del Obispo&lt;/a&gt;, covering 75.5 km.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by historical attractions. You can cycle through landscapes dotted with historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3254281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4461605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6193573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chaves Sotomayor Palace&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350466299/trujillo-and-monfraguee-national-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo and Monfragüe National Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views of Monfragüe National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for advanced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. For advanced cyclists, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350466299/trujillo-and-monfraguee-national-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo and Monfragüe National Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; present a significant challenge, spanning 172.9 km with substantial elevation gains. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058663809/trujillo-and-the-villuercas-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo and the Villuercas Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 137.2 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Trujillo?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, with pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in the open dehesa, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass by reservoirs or castles?
A: Yes, you can find routes that combine natural beauty with historical landmarks. The moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350477237/the-santa-ana-reservoir-and-the-castle-of-monroy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Santa Ana Reservoir and The Castle of Monroy&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through varied terrain including a reservoir and a historic castle.

Q: How long do road cycling routes around Trujillo typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350462516/circular-route-of-trujillo-la-aldea-del-obispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of Trujillo, La Aldea del Obispo&lt;/a&gt; might take around 2 hours 35 minutes, while a difficult and extensive route such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350466299/trujillo-and-monfraguee-national-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo and Monfragüe National Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; could take over 8 hours.

Q: What makes road cycling in Trujillo unique compared to other regions?
A: Trujillo's road cycling stands out due to its blend of historical immersion and natural beauty. Routes often weave through ancient towns, past castles, and into the unique dehesa woodlands, offering a cultural experience alongside the physical activity. The proximity to Monfragüe National Park also provides opportunities to cycle through one of the best-preserved Mediterranean mountain landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Trujillo?
A: Over 200 road cyclists have explored Trujillo's varied terrain using komoot. They often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from rolling hills to historical sites, and the well-maintained network of routes that cater to various ability levels. The opportunity to combine challenging rides with cultural stops is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Trujillo?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available around Trujillo. While many routes are moderate to difficult, beginners can find suitable options to enjoy the region's scenery at a more relaxed pace. It's always recommended to check the route details for elevation and distance to match your fitness level.
tips: Montánchez is a charming historic town in the Sierra de Montánchez region. A strategic medieval city that was disputed many times. Stop and admire its beauty at Plaza de España for a drink and a bite to eat.

The Castillo de Montánchez was a defensive castle that protected the trade routes of the Sierra de Montánchez. Spectacular views from the top of the old town and the beautiful scenery beyond. The castle was the center of many battles and passed from Christian to Arab hands on several occasions. Both leaving their architectural signature in the impressive historical monument.

The equestrian statue of Francisco Pizarro is located in Trujillo, a city in the Extremadura region, Spain. This imposing statue pays tribute to one of the illustrious residents of Trujillo: the conquistador Francisco Pizarro. The statue was created by American sculptor Charles Cary Rumsey (1879-1922). It is located in the Plaza Mayor of Trujillo, on a granite pedestal. Although not its original location, this statue is an important symbol in the city where Pizarro was born.

Trujillo Castle (Cáceres, Extremadura) is one of Spain's most emblematic fortresses, boasting a fascinating history and architecture that blends Arab and Christian influences.
Arab Origins (9th-12th centuries): It was built on Cabeza del Zorro hill during the Muslim period. The oldest remains are two Arab cisterns.
Reconquest: In 1186, Alfonso VIII captured the city, although it fell under Almohad rule before being definitively incorporated into the Crown in 1232.
Middle Ages: It played a key role in the border defense between Castile and Portugal and was the scene of conflicts such as the War of the Castilian Succession.
Modern Era: It lost its military importance and became a stately residence.
